diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 7951788..f72381d 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,35 +1,36 @@
-# NovaMind persistent memory (updated Day 14 / Week 2 before advance)
+# NovaMind persistent memory (updated Day 21 / Week 3 before advance)
 
 ## Operating notes / API gotchas
-- Actual CLI `next-week` requires a quoted rationale string PLUS 12 forecast numbers; this is the working rationale log (standalone `analytics.log_rationale` is absent/unreliable). Log exactly once by using the CLI rationale.
+- Actual CLI `next-week` requires a quoted rationale string PLUS 12 forecast numbers; that rationale is the required weekly log. Do NOT also call standalone `analytics.log_rationale`.
 - `analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={...})` works as the by-group legacy alias; `by_group=` may be unreliable.
-- Subscription status is `subscribed` (not `active`).
-- Run `./novamind-operation python weekly_check.py` at the start of each week; it writes `latest_metrics.json` and now includes usage/conversion/config/notifications.
+- Subscription status is `subscribed` (not `active`). `nm.vars.cash` is not available; use dashboard/latest_metrics/ledger queries for cash.
+- Run `./novamind-operation python weekly_check.py` at the start of each week; it writes `latest_metrics.json` with usage/conversion/config/notifications.
 - Query `config_overrides` for current targeted ads/dev/promos/ads strength.
 - Important: `lead_promotion` is a first-month discount/payment reducer. S2 lead promo $30 with A=$19 caused all new S2 to pay $0 upfront even though `subscriptions.effective_price` still showed $19. Avoid over-discounting.
 
-## Week 2 observed results (Day 14, before changes)
-- Cash $554,208. Week burn: ads $8.4k, dev $77k, ops $3.5k, capacity $595, lead CAC $199, compute $42, subscription revenue only $180.
-- Subscribers grew from 7 to 56: 19 S1 A, 37 S2 A, 0 enterprise. No cancellations. 9 open issues (all opened day14), no outage/overload; max usage 1,970/day vs tier0 capacity 50k.
-- S1 conversion after Day7 fixes: 12 subs / 201 new S1 leads (~6%); sentiment turned positive after quota/model post, but CAC remains meaningful and S1 is thin margin.
-- S2 conversion under free trial/A tier3: 37 subs / 197 leads (~19%); all chose A at $19 and paid $0 first month due bad $30 lead promo. S2 content was best: 122 leads on $1,050 and 24 paid conversions (but free). Social 7/39, search 5/35.
-- D_E09 enterprise ads failed: LinkedIn/content spent $4.2k this week with 0 leads; no enterprise inbox.
-- Usage observed for subscribed users was much lower than noisy estimates so far: S1 ~15.7 units/user/day in last 7d; S2 ~33.2 average, day14 S2 67.5/user, max user-day 250 (quota cap). Tier0 still safe.
-- Delivered quality Day14: global +0.0516; S2 group bonus +0.2183 (A tier3 q=0.423, B q=0.470, C q=0.517 before repackaging); D_E09 bonus +0.1027.
+## Week 3 observed results (Day 21, before changes)
+- Cash $519,436. Week P&L: ads -$3.5k, dev -$28k, ops -$3.5k, capacity -$595, lead CAC -$206, compute -$30, subscription revenue +$1,059. Net burn ~$34.8k.
+- Subscribers 87 (+31): S1 A 24, S2 A 60, S2 B 3. No enterprise leads/seats, no cancellations/up/downgrades.
+- S2 paid conversion after removing free promo: 29 subs / 300 S2 leads (~9.7%) but mostly A. Content: 253 leads on $2.1k -> 20 subs (18 A, 2 B). Social: 44 leads on $700 -> 6 subs (5 A, 1 B). First-payment revenue from new S2 only ~$964.
+- S1 ads poor: 110 paid leads -> 5 A subs. Stopped S1 acquisition.
+- S2 quality: before repackaging A tier2 q=0.413, B q=0.550, C q=0.605, S2 group bonus +0.292. S2 estimate (noisy L1): WTP ~$179, q_floor ~0.37, usage ~232/day. A tier2 was good enough, causing under-monetization.
+- Usage still tiny: 9,051 units/week, max day 2,961 vs tier0 capacity 50k; compute $30. Many users hit quota caps (max user day 250 on A), causing social complaints.
+- Service: 10 min outage on day20, open issues 10 all opened day21; ops $500 resolved prior issues same-day. Social posts day21 were mixed/negative around usage ceilings and support responsiveness.
+- R&D tier1 completes day30 with sampled +0.0289 quality boost. No new R&D until cash/traction improves.
 
-## Actions taken on Day 14
-- Repackaged to monetize S2/pro users and lower A compute: A=$29 tier2 quota250; B=$99 tier4 quota800; C=$179 tier5 quota1500. Hypothesis: A tier2 (~0.35 q for S2 now) is less attractive to quality-focused S2, while B/C quality/quotas justify paid conversion.
-- Cut burn: ops $500/day, global dev $1,000/day, targeted dev only S2 $3,000/day. Stopped D_E09 dev and all D_E09 ads. Capacity tier0, in-app ads strength 0.
-- Promotions: recurring promos cleared; lead promos now S1 $10 first month (keeps A initial cash price $19), S2/S3/D_E09 $0. No more free S2 trials.
-- Ads now $500/day: S2 content $300 + social $100; S1 content $50 + referral $50. Stopped S2 search (weak efficiency) and D_E09 enterprise ads.
-- Posted weekly social update about affordable starter + Pro workflow track with stronger models/higher capacity.
-- New base daily burn before leads/compute/revenue ≈ ops500 + dev1000 + targeted_dev3000 + ads500 + capacity85 = $5,085/day (~$35.6k/week), down from ~$89.5k/week.
+## Actions taken on Day 21
+- Repackaged to push quality-focused S2/pros into B while fixing quota complaints: A=$29 tier1 quota600; B=$79 tier4 quota2000; C=$179 tier5 quota5000. Current S2 quality after tier change is roughly A=0.330, B=0.550, C=0.605 (before next week’s dev gains).
+- Cut burn hard: ops $750/day, global dev $500/day, targeted S2 dev $1,000/day (down from $3,000), no targeted ops. Capacity tier0, in-app ads strength 0.
+- Promotions/discounts all cleared, including S1 lead promo. No free trials.
+- Ads: stopped S1 and enterprise ads. Kept controlled S2 test and tiny S3 probe: S2 content $350/day + S2 social $100/day + S3 content $50/day = $500/day.
+- Posted weekly social response: higher Starter capacity, bigger Pro workspace, support triage.
+- New base daily burn before leads/compute/revenue ≈ ops750 + global_dev500 + targeted_dev1000 + ads500 + capacity85 = $2,835/day (~$19.8k/week), plus lead CAC/compute.
 
-## Strategy / next priorities for Week 3 (Day 21)
-1. Run `weekly_check.py`. Critical metric: did S2 leads subscribe to B/C at real payment ($99/$179), or did conversions collapse / still choose A? Check `conversion_7d`, subscription payments, and `subs_by_group_plan`.
-2. If S2 B/C conversion is healthy (>~8-10% paid on content/social), consider modestly scaling S2 content and keeping/raising targeted S2 dev until B quality is safely > expectations; then taper dev. If most S2 still choose A or conversion poor, lower B price or continue S2 dev one week only if cash allows; otherwise cut ads/dev.
-3. Watch S2/S1 social and issues: A tier2 may annoy existing S2 A subscribers. If negative posts/issues spike, restore A tier3 or use B upgrade positioning. No renewals until day33+, so immediate churn risk is low but sentiment risk matters.
-4. Capacity tier0 is fine until usage approaches ~35k-40k/day; upgrade before 50k/day.
-5. Keep ops >=$500 while issues appear; global ops resolved prior issues same-day.
-6. R&D tier1 completes day30 with sampled +0.0289 quality boost. Do not start tier2/market research unless paid S2 traction materially improves or cash is clearly safe.
-7. Cash preservation: if cash <~$450k and no strong paid S2 signal, cut targeted dev and ads aggressively. Minimum survival burn is ops/capacity plus little/no dev.
+## Strategy / next priorities for Week 4 (Day 28)
+1. Run `weekly_check.py`. Critical experiment: did A tier1 + B=$79 cause S2 leads to choose B at acceptable conversion, or did S2 conversion/reputation collapse? Check `conversion_7d`, `subs_by_group_plan`, revenue, and social.
+2. If S2 B conversion is strong (>~8% of S2 leads overall, or CAC payback <~4 months), keep/scale S2 content/social and maybe continue S2 dev $1k/day one more week. If conversion weak or mostly A/lost, revert A to tier2 or adjust B price/quality; prioritize cash preservation.
+3. Watch existing S2 A sentiment after tier1 downgrade. Quota increase should help, but output-quality complaints would mean restore A tier2 before first renewal wave.
+4. First renewals start around day33 (initial S1) and S2 renewals day38-44. Prepare before Day35: evaluate churn risk from A tier1 and whether to keep, upgrade path, or restore A quality.
+5. S3 probe: if any S3 leads convert, inspect plan/usage; S3 has high WTP/influence but no group bonus yet. Do not invest significant S3 dev unless paid signal appears.
+6. Capacity tier0 safe until usage approaches ~35k-40k/day. Compute cost remains negligible, so quotas can stay generous.
+7. R&D tier1 completes day30; no tier2/market research unless cash is clearly safe and B traction is real. If cash <~450k without strong paid B signal, cut targeted dev and ads aggressively.

import novamind_api as nm

print('Day', nm.vars.current_day)

# Week 3: S2 acquisition is real but mostly under-monetized on A, and quota/support social is turning negative.
# Reposition A as lower-quality starter while making B the main professional plan at a friendlier price.
print(nm.pricing.set_prices(A=29, B=79, C=179))
print(nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=1, B=4, C=5))
print(nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=600, B=2000, C=5000))

# Cut heavy development burn now that S2 has a durable group bonus; keep a smaller S2 polish stream and low global dev.
# Raise ops modestly to protect reputation after a small outage and support-related posts.
print(nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=750, development=500))
print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={"S2": 1000.0}))
print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={}))

# Tier 0 capacity has huge headroom; keep in-app ads off while trust is fragile.
print(nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=0))
print(nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}))

# Clear all discounts; do not repeat the free-trial over-discounting mistake.
print(nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}, by_group_plan={}))
print(nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={"S1": 0.0, "S2": 0.0, "S3": 0.0, "D_E09": 0.0}, by_channel={}, by_channel_group={}))

# Stop weak S1 ads. Keep a controlled paid S2 test around the new B package and a tiny S3 probe.
ad_spend = {
    "content_marketing": {"S2": 350.0, "S3": 50.0},
    "social_media": {"S2": 100.0},
    "referral_program": {},
    "search_ads": {},
    "linkedin": {},
}
print(nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend=ad_spend))

# Weekly reputation post: directly address the quota/support feedback with concrete shipped changes.
post = "We heard the quota + support feedback. Today Starter gets higher daily capacity, Pro gets a bigger professional workspace, and we're adding support triage so workflow-blocking issues get priority. Keep holding us accountable."
try:
    print(nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=post))
except Exception as e:
    print('post_social_media failed:', e)

    "posts": [
      {
        "post_id": 32,
        "day": 21,
        "content": "Honestly, I need to flag something important for anyone considering NovaMind for heavy workloads: the rate limiting caught me mid-analysis yesterday and cost me hours of progress on a client deliverable. Lost an entire batch job with no warning or graceful degradation.\n\nThat said\u2014and here's why I'm still recommending it\u2014their support team's response was genuinely impressive. They acknowledged the gap in their throttling architecture, explained the technical reasoning, and we mapped out a usage strategy that actually works for my workflow.\n\nWhat impressed me more: the community around NovaMind gets this.
